candidates.evidence_quote: primarily due to an increase in comparable restaurant sales, as well as additional revenue related to new restaurant openings
Investing in new Company-owned restaurant development is our top long-term capital allocation priority

based on an increase of 10.6% in menu pricing, partially offset by 5.4% negative impact from mix
partially offset by decreased customer traffic of 3.7%

North Italia comparable sales increased approximately 8% from the second quarter of fiscal 2022

causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity and wage inflation

find suitable sites and manage increasing construction costs
lower workers compensation insurance costs due to higher claim activity in the second quarter of fiscal 2022 (0.2%)
a decline in group medical insurance costs due to lower claim activity (0.2%)
partially offset by launch cost related to our Rewards program (0.2%)

partially offset by increased manager staffing levels (0.2%)

a lower proportion of employment credits in relation to income before income taxes in the second quarter of fiscal 2023 (9.4%)
we anticipate total revenue for fiscal 2023 to be approximately $3.5 billion
we expect fiscal 2023 net income margin of approximately 4%
